projects and training undertaken: major project: experimental investigation of effect of wollostanite on impact resistance of e-glass/epoxy composites subjected to thermal ageing aircrafts are subjected to various loads, externally and internally. the loads from nature cant be stopped but we can modify the aircraft structure to resist the impact of loads. for this, the material used in aircrafts are important, they should have the ability to resist the impact without compensating for weight and strength. a small indentation can cause an aircraft structure to fail, which may lead to air crash. so impact resistance of the aircraft structure is very important in the aircraft industry. composites are having best strength to weight ratio, for impact resistance hybrid composites made of epoxy, e-glass fibers and wollostanite can be used. the method used for the composite fabrication is autoclave. the testing parameters are tensile, compression and impact resistance using utm and charpy test. the sem image is also captured for studying the internal structure and the damage. internship: hal (aircraft division), bangalore aerotrix (rc aircraft, hovercraft, ornithopters),
